at_a_glance: Promptly has cost $7.06 across 12 sessions over two days. The app shipped at $6.30 after session 11, with session 12 adding Phase 4 v2 features and then pruning two of them (F-HISTORY and F-LANGUAGE) before they shipped — keeping the codebase lean for an upcoming React migration. Costs are healthy and stable: no session exceeded $1.30, and the project is complete.

session_narratives.2026-04-18-001: Planning day — brainstorm, architect, and spec review. Three heavy reading tasks establishing BRIEF.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, and full SPEC. Expected spend for a planning session.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-002: Most expensive session — all 9 Phase 1 tasks plus phase-1-review in one run. 11 tasks at $0.12 average is efficient; cost driven by volume and a 55K-token review.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-003: Spec and planning session for F-STATE. No code committed — pure spec writing and review. Lighter than the build sessions that followed.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-004: F-STATE build — 5 tasks completing the full state machine, CSS, and IPC wiring. Efficient at $0.10/task average.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-005: F-FIRST-RUN planning and spec. Large spec write (88K input) for the first-run feature. No build tasks yet.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-006: Lightest session of the project — finished one F-FIRST-RUN task and ran vibe-cost. $0.20 for a half-session of cleanup.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-007: Dark glass design implementation plus 6 BUG-002 visual fixes. The large design output (10K tokens) drove the output cost up.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-008: DECISION-004 (recording refactor from pillWin to main window) plus BUG-004 vibrancy fixes. Architecture-level refactor required deep reading.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-009: Heaviest Phase 2 session — vibrancy fixes across two rounds, BUG-007 RAF loop, and the full FEATURE-001 splash screen. $0.68 for 6 tasks is reasonable given the scope.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-010: Phase 2 review (L task, 80K input tokens) plus all BL-013–023 fixes. The phase-2-review alone was $0.31 — expected for a full codebase audit.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-011: Final review reading 9 source files, writing the gate report, RFX-001 console cleanup, and vibe-cost. Light session at $0.38 — the project is shipped.
session_narratives.2026-04-19-012: Phase 4 v2 features session: completed the parallel build (dark mode, tray, history, language), then removed F-HISTORY and F-LANGUAGE before shipping to keep the codebase clean for the planned React migration. Session cost slightly elevated by continuation overhead from a compacted prior session.

recommendations.title: Prompt caching never activated across all sessions
recommendations.body: Cache hit rate was 0% throughout the entire project — every session loaded CLAUDE.md and CODEBASE.md from scratch. For a next project, keeping CLAUDE.md stable would let Anthropic cache the large static context. Cache reads cost 90% less than fresh reads.

recommendations.title: Session continuation adds ~40K token overhead
recommendations.body: Sessions that start from a compacted prior session load a large summary upfront (~40K tokens). Keeping sessions shorter (under ~80K tokens total) avoids compaction entirely and eliminates this overhead. Session 12 paid ~$0.12 just for the continuation context.
recommendations.saving: save ~$0.10–0.15 per session started from compaction

forecast_narrative: Project is complete at $7.06. The React migration is the next major investment — expect $8–14 total based on the current rate of ~$0.15/task average. A typical migration of this scope (15–25 M-sized React component tasks) would run $2.70–$4.50 in build cost alone, plus planning.
